our_argument_summary: Our review says Tesla patent 787412 contains the time value 0.08484 s which converts to ~11.78 Hz, but that the specific formula f=c/(2D) framed as a disc-resonance formula does not appear in the patent. Tesla was describing spherical-Earth propagation, not flat-disc standing-wave resonance. We also flag the incompatibility between WIN-001 (11.78 Hz) and WIN-002 (7.83 Hz Schumann).

holes_found.description: Our review says Tesla 'measured' a propagation time of 0.08484 s. The patent actually says 'which I have estimated to be not less than one-twelfth or probably 0.08484 of a second.' This is an estimate/calculation, not a direct measurement. The word 'measured' overstates the empirical basis. A smart defender could seize on this to claim we're mischaracterizing Tesla.
holes_found.recommendation: Change 'Tesla measured a propagation time' to 'Tesla estimated a propagation time' in the finding field. This is more accurate to the patent text and actually makes our point stronger: the dome model is building on Tesla's theoretical estimate, not an empirical measurement.

holes_found.description: Our review says Tesla described 'pulse propagation timing through a spherical Earth.' The patent actually describes propagation 'over the earth's surface' at 471,240 km/s (≈1.57c), which is surface wave propagation, not through the interior. The phrase 'through a spherical Earth' is slightly misleading. Tesla's calculation uses the circumference (round trip over surface ≈ 40,000 km at 1.57c), not 2× diameter at c, though these give nearly identical results due to the mathematical identity 1.57c/circumference ≈ c/(2×diameter) since 1.57 ≈ π/2.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider clarifying to 'surface wave propagation around a spherical Earth' rather than 'through.' This is more precise and actually blocks a potential defender argument that Tesla wasn't describing interior propagation.

holes_found.description: We don't address what 'disc_thickness' actually means in the dome model. The dome's own claim says f = c/(2 × disc_thickness) = 11.787 Hz. Solving: disc_thickness = c/(2×11.787) = 12,717 km. This is Earth's diameter (12,742 km), NOT any dome parameter (disc radius = 20,015 km, firmament height = 9,086 km, disc diameter = 40,030 km). The dome model's 'disc thickness' is literally just the globe's diameter by another name, but our review never makes this devastating point. A smart defender could dodge our current rebuttal by saying 'disc_thickness is a dome-specific structural parameter.' We should show that no such parameter exists — it's Earth's diameter smuggled in.
holes_found.recommendation: Add to detail_evidence: 'The formula f = c/(2D) = 11.787 Hz requires D = 12,717 km — which is Earth's diameter (12,742 km), not any dome structural parameter. The dome's disc radius is 20,015 km, its firmament height is 9,086 km, and its disc diameter is 40,030 km. None of these yield 11.78 Hz. The model obtains the correct frequency only by using the globe's diameter and relabeling it "disc thickness."'

holes_found.description: The dome model's live site (current V51.0) appears to have evolved beyond the simple f=c/(2D) formula. WebFetch of john09289.github.io/predictions shows WIN-001 now uses a more complex ovoid cavity resonance formula: fn = (va/2πa)√(n(n+1)·Htotal/H(r)), with va = 1.57c and a = 20,000 km. Our review still addresses the simpler formula from the raw text capture. If the dome site has moved the goalposts, our review may be criticizing an outdated version of the claim.
holes_found.recommendation: Verify whether the dome site has actually updated WIN-001's formula. If so, our review needs to address the newer formulation (which may still have the same fundamental issue — using globe-derived parameters). Note: our raw-text/02-wins.txt still shows the simple formula, so the update may have happened after our text capture.

stronger_arguments.description: The mathematical identity exposing the relabeling trick: Tesla used v=471,240 km/s over Earth's circumference. Since 471,240/c ≈ π/2, we get v·t = (π/2)c × (πD)/((π/2)c) = circumference. The dome's f=c/(2D) is algebraically equivalent but disguises the spherical origin. Explicitly showing this equivalence — and that 'disc_thickness' must equal Earth's diameter — is a more precise and devastating critique than simply saying Tesla assumed a sphere.
sources: Mathematical derivation: 1.57c/circumference = c/(2×diameter) because 1.57≈π/2 and circumference=π×diameter

stronger_arguments.description: The velocity 471,240 km/s is itself a globe-model parameter. It represents the phase velocity of ELF waves in the spherical Earth-ionosphere waveguide (~1.57c). This superluminal phase velocity arises specifically from waveguide geometry — concentric spherical shells. A flat disc cavity would have different waveguide modes and a different phase velocity. So even the velocity constant the dome borrows (which it calls 'Tesla Longitudinal Constant') is a spherical-geometry result.
sources: Corum & Aidinejad 1986, 'Transient Propagation of ELF Pulses in the Earth Ionosphere Cavity'

stronger_arguments.description: The WIN-001/WIN-002 incompatibility deserves sharper framing. The Schumann resonance (7.83 Hz) is a transverse electromagnetic resonance of the spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity. The 11.78 Hz is a longitudinal body-wave/surface-wave resonance related to Earth's diameter. These are physically distinct phenomena with different mechanisms. The dome model counts both as 'confirmed predictions' of a single cavity, but any single cavity has ONE fundamental resonance — not two unrelated ones at 7.83 and 11.78 Hz. The model never derives both from the same geometry.
sources: Besser 2007, 'Synopsis of the historical development of Schumann resonances', Radio Science

kernel_of_truth: 11.78 Hz is a genuine electromagnetic frequency associated with Earth's dimensions. Tesla really did calculate this value in his 1905 patent, estimating the round-trip surface propagation time as 0.08484 seconds. The reciprocal gives ~11.79 Hz, which represents a real physical resonance of the Earth system — specifically the coherence time of the Earth-ionosphere cavity. The dome model correctly identifies this as an important electromagnetic frequency and correctly traces it to Tesla. What it gets wrong is the geometry: the frequency derives from spherical Earth parameters (diameter 12,742 km or equivalently circumference at superluminal phase velocity), not from any flat-disc structural dimension.
deeper_analysis:
Tesla's US Patent 787,412 (1905) describes electrical energy transmission through 'the natural mediums,' treating Earth as a resonant conductor. The patent states that the wave train should continue for 'not less than one-twelfth or probably 0.08484 of a second,' which Tesla characterizes as the time for signals to travel 'to and returning from the region diametrically opposite the pole over the earth's surface with a mean velocity of about four hundred and seventy-one thousand two hundred and forty kilometers per second.' This language unambiguously describes surface-wave propagation around a spherical Earth — 'diametrically opposite the pole' is meaningless on a flat disc where no geometric pole-to-antipode relationship exists.

The dome model's claim that f = c/(2 × disc_thickness) appears in the patent is straightforwardly false — no such formula exists in the document. However, 1/0.08484 does yield 11.787 Hz, so the frequency value genuinely traces to Tesla. The deeper issue our review should emphasize is the geometric impossibility: the formula f = c/(2D) = 11.787 Hz requires D = 12,717 km. This matches Earth's diameter (12,742 km) to within 0.2%, but corresponds to NO parameter in the dome model. The dome's disc radius is 20,015 km, its firmament apex height is 9,086 km, and its disc diameter is 40,030 km. The number 12,717 km is the globe's diameter and nothing else. By calling it 'disc_thickness,' the dome model performs a terminological relabeling of a globe parameter.

Furthermore, Tesla's velocity of 471,240 km/s (≈1.572c) is itself a spherical-geometry result. It represents the phase velocity of ELF waves in the Earth-ionosphere waveguide — a property that arises specifically from concentric spherical shell geometry. The mathematical relationship 471,240/c ≈ π/2 is not coincidental: it reflects the ratio of the half-circumference (the surface path to the antipode) to the diameter (the straight-line distance through the interior). A flat-disc cavity would exhibit fundamentally different waveguide modes and characteristic velocities.

The incompatibility with WIN-002 deserves emphasis. WIN-001 claims 11.78 Hz as the fundamental cavity resonance; WIN-002 claims 7.83 Hz (Schumann) as deriving from the same cavity with '26% aetheric damping.' But 7.83 Hz with 26% damping removed gives ~10.6 Hz, not 11.78 Hz. These are two distinct physical phenomena — Schumann resonance (transverse EM cavity mode) and Tesla's body-wave resonance (longitudinal propagation) — that the dome model conflates as if they're related manifestations of the same disc geometry.

Our current review is substantially correct in verdict and reasoning. The primary improvement opportunities are: (1) noting that Tesla 'estimated' rather than 'measured' the 0.08484 s value, (2) specifying that it's surface propagation rather than through-the-interior propagation, (3) adding the devastating quantitative point that 'disc_thickness' = 12,717 km matches only Earth's diameter, and (4) noting that the dome's live site may have updated to a more complex formula that still smuggles in globe parameters.
